title: Classic repeating pattern abcabcbb 🔁
description: The longest substring without duplicates is abc, so the maximum length is 3 ✅

title: Single repeated character bbbbb 🧱
description: Every window longer than one character repeats b, so the answer is 1 🔒

title: Mixed repeat with valid middle window pwwkew 🎯
description: The longest valid substring is wke, which has length 3 ✨

title: Empty string edge case ∅
description: An empty string has no substrings, so the longest valid length is 0 📭

title: Single character input a 🔤
description: A one-character string is always a valid substring of length 1 👍

title: Duplicate appears after a long clean run abca 🚪
description: The longest unique substring is abc before the second a forces the window to move 🚶

title: All characters unique abcdef 🚀
description: Since no character repeats, the whole string is the answer 🌟

title: Repeated block with reset dvdf 🔄
description: The best substring is vdf, which shows the window should continue after removing the duplicate 🔍

title: Spaces count as characters a b c ␠
description: Spaces are part of the string too, and the longest duplicate-free substring has length 3 🪟

title: Symbols and punctuation stay valid !@#$%^ 🔣
description: Special characters are allowed, and all six are unique here 💥

title: Case sensitivity matters aA 👀
description: Lowercase a and uppercase A are different characters, so both can stay in the window 🔠

title: Immediate repeat after progress abba ⚠️
description: The longest valid substrings are ab and ba, each with length 2 ↔️

title: Longest window near the end tmmzuxt 📍
description: After handling repeated m, the longest unique substring becomes mzuxt with length 5 🏁

title: Numbers behave like normal characters 12345123 🔢
description: The longest duplicate-free substring is 12345 before digits begin repeating 📈

title: Longest window starts after duplicates anviaj 🧭
description: After the repeated a is handled, the best window becomes nviaj with length 5 🌈

title: Repeat forces a tight jump in the middle ohomm 🪜
description: The best windows is hom, so the longest valid length is 3 🎯

title: Trailing duplicate trims the answer abcdeaf 🔚
description: The best substring is bcdeaf, which keeps six unique characters after the repeated a shifts the window ➡️

title: Alternating duplicates keep the window moving abababab 🔄
description: Only ab and ba stay valid, so the longest duplicate-free length remains 2 🔁

title: Long clean stretch after an early repeat cddabcde 🛣️
description: Once the duplicate d is cleared, the window grows to abcde with length 5 🚀

title: Repeated spaces matter just like letters a  b  c ␠
description: Consecutive spaces are duplicates too, so the longest valid window reaches only length 2 🧩

title: Unique substring made of symbols and letters a!b@c#d$ ✨
description: Every character is unique here, so the entire mixed string is the answer 🎉

title: Duplicate returns after a gap abccdefgh 🧱
description: After the repeated c resets the window, cdefgh becomes the longest valid substring 🌟

title: Window grows to the full string through mixed case aAbBcC 🔠
description: Uppercase and lowercase letters are distinct, so all six characters can stay together ✅

title: Many repeats before a clean finish zzzabcdef 🏁
description: The repeated z values collapse early windows, then zabcdef stretches to length 7 💨

title: Duplicate at both ends creates a long inner answer abcdefga 🔍
description: The repeated a at the end shifts the window, but bcdefga still gives a longest length of 7 🪄

title: Short tricky pattern pkewp 📌
description: The longest substring without repeats is kewp, which reaches length 4 after the first p drops out 🚪

title: Late repeat after full progress qwertyq ⌨️
description: The first six characters are all unique, and the final q only matches the opening one at the end 🧠
